concepts.finding: CGANs condition both generator and discriminator on auxiliary information (class labels), enabling controlled generation of class-specific samples and improving training stability.
concepts.implication: Conditioning on auxiliary information (user features, context, session history) can improve both generation and discrimination, applicable to search ranking conditioned on user context.
concepts.open_question: Which user context signals are most valuable for conditioning search ranking models?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A ranking model conditioned on user context (device, location, time, past behavior) will outperform an unconditioned model by 8%+ NDCG on personalized search tasks.
